title: lepidonia corae
intro: Lepidonia corae, also known as the 'White Guava', is a shrub-like plant native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and saponins. The health benefits of Lepidonia corae include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of Lepidonia corae are typically made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
